title: Curry Lovers: Global Pots & Weeknight Bases
description: A globe-trotting curry set—Caribbean to West African to Indian, Thai, and beyond. Pantry-first, no-pork, freezer-friendly where it counts, and ready for your weekly flavor rhythm.

meta.curation_notes: Favor 30–40 min weeknights with a few batchable bases. Keep heat adjustable and sides flexible (rice, roti, naan, quinoa, cassava).
meta.serving_plan: Pair with rice, flatbreads, or steamed greens. Portion & freeze where noted.
meta.storage_policy: 2–4 days fridge (≤4°C). 2–3 months freezer in airtight, labeled portions.

items.title: Trini Channa & Aloo (Weeknight)
items.description: Trinidad-style curry chickpeas and potatoes—bright, cozy, meatless.
steps: Sauté onion, garlic, ginger in oil 3–4 min.
Bloom curry powder 30 sec.
Add chickpeas, potatoes, liquid; simmer 15–18 min until tender.
Finish with herbs; season to taste.

items.title: Jamaican Curry Chicken (Lean, No Pork)
items.description: Classic Jamaican curry profile on chicken; weeknight-friendly.
steps: Brown seasoned chicken 3–4 min; set aside.
Sauté onion/garlic/pepper; bloom curry 30 sec.
Return chicken; add coconut milk/liquid, thyme; simmer 15–20 min to tender; season.

items.title: West African Groundnut Stew (Quick)
items.description: Peanut-tomato curry with greens; gentle chili heat and deep comfort.
ingredients: Chili flake or fresh chili (to taste)
steps: Sauté onion/garlic 3–4 min; add tomato paste 1 min.
Whisk in peanut butter and liquid; simmer 10 min.
Add greens; simmer 3–5 min; season.

items.title: Thai Green Curry (Weeknight, Buildable Heat)
items.description: Fast green-curry broth with vegetables and your choice of protein.
ingredients: 2 cups mixed veg (zucchini, bell pepper, green beans)
1–2 tsp fish sauce or soy/coconut aminos
steps: Bloom curry paste in oil 30–60 sec.
Add coconut milk and liquid; simmer 5 min.
Add veg + protein; simmer 6–8 min until tender.
Finish with lime and herbs; season.

items.title: Simple North-Indian Chicken Curry
items.description: Onion–tomato–garam masala base; reliable and flexible.
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil or ghee (or neutral oil)
steps: Sauté onions to deep golden 10–12 min; add garlic/ginger 1 min.
Add spices; toast 30 sec. Stir in tomatoes; simmer 5 min.
Add chicken; simmer 15–20 min until done; finish with garam masala & cilantro.

items.title: Japanese Kare (Pressure Cooker or Pot)
items.description: Gentle, kid-friendly Japanese curry with carrots and potatoes.
ingredients: 3–4 tbsp Japanese curry roux or homemade blend
steps: Sauté onion 3–4 min; add veg (and meat if using).
Add broth; pressure cook 8 min (or simmer pot 20–25 min).
Stir in curry roux to thicken; season.

items.title: Malaysian Laksa (Lite Weeknight)
items.description: Fragrant laksa broth with noodles; use tofu, shrimp, or chicken.

meta.storage: Broth 3 days fridge / 2 months freezer; cook noodles fresh

steps: Bloom laksa paste in oil 30–60 sec; add coconut milk & broth.
Simmer 8–10 min; add protein to heat through.
Serve over noodles; top with sprouts, herbs, lime.

items.title: South Indian Egg Curry
items.description: Coconut-tomato gravy with boiled eggs—budget-friendly and filling.
steps: Sauté onion/ginger/garlic; add mustard seeds/curry leaves if using.
Add chili/turmeric; stir tomatoes and coconut milk.
Simmer 8–10 min; add eggs to warm; season.

items.title: Thai Massaman (Slow or Pressure)
items.description: Warm, gently spiced curry with potatoes—beef or chicken.
ingredients: 1 tbsp fish sauce or soy/coconut aminos
steps: Bloom paste in oil; add coconut milk & broth.
Add meat and veg; pressure cook 15 min (or simmer 35–45).
Season with fish sauce/soy and sugar to balance.

items.title: Caribbean Curry Fish Stew (Light)
items.description: Bright, citrusy curry broth with lean fish and peppers.
steps: Sauté onion/pepper; bloom curry 30 sec.
Add coconut milk/liquid; simmer 5 min.
Add fish; simmer gently 4–6 min; finish with lime and cilantro.

items.title: Quick Veggie Korma (Lite)
items.description: Creamy cashew-coconut korma with mixed vegetables.
ingredients: 2 tsp korma or mild curry powder
1/2 cup cashews, blended with 1 cup water
steps: Sauté onion/garlic/ginger; add korma powder 30 sec.
Stir in cashew blend & coconut milk; simmer.
Add veg; cook until tender; season.

items.title: Green Curry Paste (Base, Freezer Cubes)
items.description: Homemade paste to jump-start Thai green curries.
ingredients: 2 cups green herbs (cilantro stems, basil, green onion)
steps: Blend smooth adding a splash of water if needed.
Portion into ice cube trays; freeze and bag.

items.title: Caribbean Green Seasoning (Curry-Ready)
items.description: Herb–garlic base to finish Caribbean curries and grills.
steps: Blend to a spoonable paste; adjust salt & lime.
Use as finishing spoon or marinade.

items.title: Indian Masala Base (Onion–Tomato)
items.description: Batch a quart to speed any Indian-style curry.

meta.reheat: Use from thaw or frozen pucks

steps: Slowly brown onions in oil until deep golden.
Add garlic/ginger; spices; tomatoes. Simmer 15–20 min.
Cool; blend smooth if desired; portion & freeze.

items.title: Laksa Paste (Shortcut, Freezer)
items.description: Aromatic base for Malaysian laksa or noodle soups.
ingredients: Zest of 1 lime or kaffir leaves
steps: Blend into a paste; sauté portions 1–2 min before adding liquids.
Freeze as cubes.

items.title: Crispy Shallots (Curry Topper)
items.description: Crunchy topper for curries, soups, and rice bowls.
steps: Fry shallots in oil over medium until golden; drain on rack; salt lightly.
Reserve flavored oil for cooking.
